opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on intraday pullbacks into 152-160 that hold above 148 with volume confirming buyers on the retest of the gap zone.
opportunities.rationale: Oracle gapped ~9–10% higher after beating Q3 FY26 estimates and posting strong cloud and backlog metrics, with shares closing near 163 after heavy post-earnings volume. A controlled pullback into the 152–160 area offers a classic gap-follow-through entry toward the mid-170s, provided the gap low near 148 continues to act as support.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Build a position on pullbacks into 193-202 that respect recent support; exit all positions no later than the close on 17 Mar, ahead of the 18 Mar pre-market report.
opportunities.rationale: Williams-Sonoma is scheduled to report Q4/FY25 results before the open on 18 Mar, with the street looking for solid EPS and resilient margins. The stock trades near the upper half of its 52-week range and historically sees pre-earnings accumulation; the edge is a short 2–3 day drift higher into the print while avoiding binary risk.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on dips into 410-430 over 15–18 Mar if price holds above 395 and the broader semi complex is not breaking down; exit fully before the 18 Mar close.
opportunities.rationale: Micron reports in the 18 Mar cluster and has been trading with elevated volatility, rebounding strongly ahead of earnings. Consensus expects healthy YoY growth and investors are focused on AI and HBM demand; this creates scope for a short pre-earnings run-up, but the high beta justifies tighter stops and zero exposure into the print.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on consolidation or shallow dips into 132-137 between 15–18 Mar if price holds above 128; exit all positions before the 19 Mar pre-market earnings release.
opportunities.rationale: Alibaba will release December-quarter results before the US open on 19 Mar and has recently sold off from the 150–160 area into the mid-130s despite upbeat longer-term analyst targets. The setup is a short pre-earnings re-rating trade from oversold levels, sized modestly given China macro and geopolitical overhangs.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Build a position on dips into 195-205 over 15–18 Mar if price holds above 188 and broader IT services are not selling off aggressively; exit fully before the 19 Mar report.
opportunities.rationale: Accenture trades near multi-year lows after a sustained de-rating despite solid fundamentals and large-scale digital transformation exposure. With earnings on 19 Mar and expectations already reset, there is room for a short pre-earnings mean-reversion bounce, but macro and cycle uncertainty keep conviction at moderate and holding period short.
